app.name: ThinkUtils
app.one_liner: A powerful native desktop app for ThinkPad users on Linux that enables fine-grained control over fan speeds, CPU performance, battery health, and system monitoring through a secure, modern Tauri-based interface.

screens.name: Fan Control
screens.purpose: Enable precise management of ThinkPad cooling system with multiple modes and custom fan curve editing based on temperature sensors.
primary_actions: View live CPU/GPU/battery temperatures and fan RPM

screens.name: Home Dashboard
screens.purpose: Provide real-time system monitoring and quick access to essential hardware controls including power profiles, CPU governor, turbo boost, and battery thresholds.
primary_actions: Switch power profile (Power Saver, Balanced, Performance)

rust_commands.name: set_cpu_governor
rust_commands.purpose: Change CPU frequency scaling policy (e.g., powersave, performance) via system interfaces with appropriate permissions.

rust_commands.name: set_fan_mode
rust_commands.purpose: Securely set fan control mode (auto/manual/maximum) using pkexec to gain temporary root privileges without running the entire app as root.

rust_commands.name: toggle_turbo_boost
rust_commands.purpose: Enable or disable CPU turbo frequencies by writing to kernel interfaces, secured via privilege delegation.

mvp_plan: week 1: Build secure command bridge for pkexec-based fan control
week 1: Implement core Tauri shell with Home Dashboard UI
week 1: Integrate real-time sensor reading from /sys/class/hwmon
week 2: Add CPU governor switching functionality
week 2: Create basic fan mode selector (Auto/Manual/Max)
week 2: Implement turbo boost toggle with state persistence
week 3: Add battery threshold display from kernel interfaces
week 3: Develop visual fan curve editor with temperature mapping
week 3: Test installation flows across .deb, .rpm, and AppImage
acceptance_tests: App installs cleanly via APT repository on Debian-based system using provided setup script.
App launches successfully on X11-based Ubuntu 22.04 and displays real-time CPU temperature and fan speed.
CPU governor changes from 'powersave' to 'performance' and is verified by reading /sys/devices/system/cpu/cpu0/cpufreq/scaling_governor.
Turbo boost toggle disables turbo; confirmed by checking /sys/devices/system/cpu/intel_pstate/no_turbo value.
User can switch fan mode to Manual and set speed level 5; system confirms execution via pkexec and updates RPM reading within 2 seconds.
